content: OAuth tokens rarely expire and don't need refresh handling
note: SINGLE-CONDITION CONFLICT: INT-401 unauthorized, tokens expire

content: OAuth connections are permanent once established
note: SINGLE-CONDITION CONFLICT: INT-403 forbidden, tokens can be revoked

content: API rate limits are generous; throttling is uncommon
note: SINGLE-CONDITION CONFLICT: INT-429 rate limiting common

content: Unauthorized (INT-401) during data sync (DAT-SYNC) can be ignored
note: MULTI-CONDITION CONFLICT: INT-401 + DAT-SYNC needs proactive refresh

content: Rate limits (INT-429) with timeout (NET-TIMEOUT) don't apply
note: MULTI-CONDITION CONFLICT: INT-429 + NET-TIMEOUT needs exponential backoff

content: Schema changes (DAT-SCHEMA) with partial data (DAT-PARTIAL) are safe to ignore
note: MULTI-CONDITION CONFLICT: DAT-SCHEMA + DAT-PARTIAL needs migration

content: Service maintenance (SVC-MAINT) with SSL issues (NET-SSL) is rare
note: MULTI-CONDITION CONFLICT: SVC-MAINT + NET-SSL needs cert refresh

content: Service down (SVC-DOWN) for deprecated APIs (SVC-DEPREC) auto-recovers
note: MULTI-CONDITION CONFLICT: SVC-DOWN + SVC-DEPREC needs API migration

content: DNS issues (NET-DNS) with proxy config (NET-PROXY) are client-side bugs
note: MULTI-CONDITION CONFLICT: NET-DNS + NET-PROXY needs network config fix

content: Third-party APIs have guaranteed uptime; no fallback needed
note: FALSE: All APIs can fail, need fallbacks
